name: The Farmers State Bank
description_short: The Farmers State Bank is a community bank headquartered in New Madison, Ohio, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with a focus on agricultural lending and local service.
description_long:
The Farmers State Bank operates as a traditional community bank serving the New Madison, Ohio area and surrounding regions, including a newer branch location at The Republic Banking Center in Republic, Ohio. The bank was established to serve both personal and agricultural customers in rural Ohio communities. The institution has evolved to offer modern banking services while maintaining a local, relationship-based approach to lending and customer service.

The bank provides a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including multiple checking and savings account options, certificates of deposit, money market accounts, personal and business loans, agricultural real estate loans, auto financing, mortgages, and recently expanded into credit card offerings. They operate online banking, bill pay, and electronic statement services available 24/7. Notably, their Student Checking Account includes a unique feature where the bank pays cash rewards for good grades, and they offer specialized Vacation Club Savings accounts.

What distinguishes The Farmers State Bank is its explicit focus on agricultural lending and rural community banking. Their website emphasizes agricultural real estate loans with flexible structures "designed around your unique operational needs." The bank also targets specific customer segments including students, small business owners, and families, with tailored products for each demographic. Their newest branch opening and continued expansion suggests active growth in their market.

As a community bank, The Farmers State Bank operates with limited geographic reach compared to national institutions, which may restrict accessibility for customers outside their service areas. While they advertise competitive rates, no comprehensive rate comparison data is provided on the website. The bank's accessibility features (AudioEye enabled) demonstrate commitment to inclusive service, though like most regional banks, they lack the technological sophistication and service breadth of larger national competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal Checking Accounts (including basic and interest-bearing options)
Student Checking Account with grade-based cash rewards
Business Direct Checking for small business owners
Savings Accounts with multiple goal-based options
Certificates of Deposit (9-month terms advertised at 3.60% APY)
Platinum Harvest Money Market Savings Account
Personal and Business Loans with competitive rates
Agricultural Real Estate Loans with flexible structures
Auto Loans with competitive rates and flexible repayment
Fixed Rate Mortgages (30-year term at 6.333% APR advertised)
Credit Cards (multiple types including cash back, rewards, travel, credit-building, and business cards)
pros: Agricultural lending specialization with flexible real estate loan structures tailored to farm operations
Student Checking Account with grade-based cash rewards incentivizing academic performance
Competitive CD rates (3.60% APY on 9-month CDs) and multiple savings products for different goals
Free 24/7 online banking, bill pay, and electronic statements with no monthly fees mentioned
Expanded credit card offerings including cash back, rewards, and business cards launched recently
Local community banking with personal loan officer support and relationship-based service
AudioEye accessibility features demonstrating commitment to inclusive banking access
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only two confirmed branch locations (New Madison and Republic, OH) restricting customer accessibility
Mortgage rate advertised (6.333% APR) is higher than current national averages, requiring rate comparison with competitors
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, minimum balance requirements for most accounts, and comprehensive rate information
No mention of FDIC insurance limits, fraud protection specifics, or detailed account terms and conditions
Limited product innovation compared to national banks and neobanks (e.g., no mention of investment services, wealth management, or advanced fintech features)

contact.address: 101 S Main St, New Madison, OH 45346

best_for: Agricultural businesses and farm owners seeking flexible real estate financing and operational credit
Families and students in the New Madison/Republic, Ohio area wanting local community banking relationships
Small business owners needing Business Direct Checking and local loan officer support for growth financing
Conservative savers seeking CDs and money market accounts from a stable community institution
diagnosis: The Farmers State Bank is best for agricultural customers and rural Ohio residents seeking personalized community banking with specialized farm lending. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside the New Madison and Republic, Ohio service areas cannot access this bank's services, and rates should be compared against national competitors before committing to mortgages or CDs.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 101 S Main St, New Madison, OH 45346
meta_description: Community bank headquartered in New Madison, Ohio offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with focus on agricultural lending.
